description: General blood screening often used for fatigue, weakness, infections, and routine wellness checks.
Blood sugar screening commonly suggested for frequent urination, unusual thirst, and suspected diabetes.
Long-term blood sugar test used to monitor diabetes control over the last few months.
Rapid blood test often recommended for fever, chills, body aches, and suspected malaria infection.
Microscopy test used to confirm malaria parasites when symptoms strongly suggest infection.
Pregnancy blood test commonly used for early pregnancy confirmation and hormone monitoring.
Urine screening often used for burning urination, lower abdominal discomfort, and possible urinary tract infection.
Thyroid hormone screening used for unexplained weight changes, tiredness, and suspected thyroid imbalance.
Early pregnancy ultrasound used to confirm pregnancy location, viability, and gestational age.
Detailed pregnancy scan used to assess fetal anatomy and development during pregnancy.
Advanced pregnancy ultrasound that provides live 4D imaging for fetal development and bonding.
Pelvic imaging commonly suggested for lower abdominal pain, menstrual concerns, and reproductive health checks.
Kidney ultrasound often used for flank pain, urinary symptoms, and suspected kidney stones or obstruction.
Chest ultrasound used to assess fluid, inflammation, and selected respiratory concerns.
Breast imaging used for breast pain, lumps, and follow-up evaluation of breast changes.
Chest X-ray often recommended for persistent cough, chest pain, fever, and breathing concerns.
Lumbar spine X-ray used to assess lower back pain, posture issues, and suspected spinal injury.
Detailed lower back imaging used for persistent lumbar pain, nerve symptoms, and disc evaluation.
Brain MRI is commonly suggested for severe headaches, seizures, dizziness, and neurological symptoms.
Joint MRI used for sports injuries, swelling, locking, and persistent knee pain.
Comprehensive imaging option used for advanced screening and broad whole-body assessment.
Detailed chest CT used for persistent cough, lung disease evaluation, and difficult respiratory cases.
Fast brain scan often recommended for head injury, sudden weakness, or emergency neurological symptoms.
CT scan focused on the kidneys, ureters, and bladder for stone pain and urinary obstruction.
Broad imaging option for extensive screening and selected whole-body diagnostic workups.
Upper GI endoscopy used for persistent ulcer symptoms, reflux, heartburn, and upper abdominal pain.
Colon examination used for rectal bleeding, bowel habit changes, and colorectal screening.
Airway endoscopy used for stubborn cough, unexplained breathing symptoms, and lung evaluation.
Basic heart rhythm test often used for chest pain, palpitations, dizziness, and cardiac screening.
24-hour heart monitor used when palpitations or fainting happen on and off through the day.
Cardiac ultrasound used to assess heart structure, pumping function, and selected chest symptoms.
Court-admissible DNA testing for legal paternity confirmation and official documentation.
Embassy-ready DNA test used to support immigration and family relationship verification.
Genetic testing used to explore family roots, ancestry background, and ethnicity insights.

Cervical screening test used for routine women's health checks and abnormal discharge follow-up.
Prostate screening blood test often suggested for urinary symptoms and men's health checks.
Brain wave test commonly used for seizures, blackouts, sleep issues, and suspected epilepsy.
Overnight monitoring used for snoring, sleep apnea, and unexplained daytime sleepiness.
